title: Bounded Node Upload Admission and Quarantine · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds Node upload-admission workflow, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Node upload-admission workflow while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Upload Byte Time Count Budget
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9973 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate upload byte and concurrency budgets before a quarantined state transition; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Upload Byte Time Count Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Upload Byte Time Count Budget, keep Upload Byte Time Count Budget connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
